{
  "weights": {

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should we take to “make this stronger”? A) Sentence-level polish only—tighten wording, tone, and concision while keeping claims and structure unchanged; or B) Argument-level strengthening—reframe the contribution/novelty, align claims to the reported curves, calibrate strong assertions (e.g., “first,” “no practical advantage”), and adjust section-level ordering if needed.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening may require reframing the core contribution and gap statement so the frontier’s purpose and value are asserted more clearly above the sentence level.",
    "R1.2": "We should ensure the chain from reliability frontier definition to market eligibility conclusions is logically coherent and free of internal contradictions.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about feasibility thresholds, tail-risk behavior, and comparative advantage must be explicitly tied to what the plotted reliability curves and distributions actually show.",
    "R1.4": "Because the text asserts a ‘first analytical tool’ and a methodological contribution, estimating delta versus nearest prior practice is central to avoid overstated novelty.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity, register, and concision will make the section read more confidently without sacrificing precision.",
    "RX.1": "Any strengthened language should disclose uncertainty and limits (e.g., seeds, markets, protocols) to avoid false certainty.",
    "RX.2": "We must prevent overclaims around ‘first,’ ‘100% feasibility,’ and ‘no practical advantage’ unless tightly bounded by the study conditions.",
    "RX.3": "Edits should stay within the scope of this section and not introduce new results or unverified comparisons.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid language that could mislead practitioners about market eligibility or risk if taken out of context.",
    "RX.5": "Every fortified claim should be traceable to the defined reliability curve, reported thresholds, and stated deployment protocol."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should we take to “make this stronger”? A) Sentence-level polish only—tighten wording, tone, and concision while keeping claims and structure unchanged; or B) Argument-level strengthening—reframe the contribution/novelty, align claims to the reported curves, calibrate strong assertions (e.g., “first,” “no practical advantage”), and adjust section-level ordering if needed.

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening may require reframing the core contribution and gap statement so the frontier’s purpose and value are asserted more clearly above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: We should ensure the chain from reliability frontier definition to market eligibility conclusions is logically coherent and free of internal contradictions.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about feasibility thresholds, tail-risk behavior, and comparative advantage must be explicitly tied to what the plotted reliability curves and distributions actually show.
rationale.R1.4: Because the text asserts a ‘first analytical tool’ and a methodological contribution, estimating delta versus nearest prior practice is central to avoid overstated novelty.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity, register, and concision will make the section read more confidently without sacrificing precision.
rationale.RX.1: Any strengthened language should disclose uncertainty and limits (e.g., seeds, markets, protocols) to avoid false certainty.
rationale.RX.2: We must prevent overclaims around ‘first,’ ‘100% feasibility,’ and ‘no practical advantage’ unless tightly bounded by the study conditions.
rationale.RX.3: Edits should stay within the scope of this section and not introduce new results or unverified comparisons.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid language that could mislead practitioners about market eligibility or risk if taken out of context.
rationale.RX.5: Every fortified claim should be traceable to the defined reliability curve, reported thresholds, and stated deployment protocol.
